paper_title: Implementation and Evaluation of an Image Analysis System for Determining Viability of Pollen Grains in Temperate Rice

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting and preparing rice panicle samples for pollen viability analysis. It involves collecting panicles, extracting anthers, and preparing them for microscopic examination.

steps.description: Panicle samples were collected from research plots subjected to different treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: collect panicle samples from research plots

steps.description: Panicles were collected early in the morning, put in paper bags, and transported in insulated coolers to the laboratory.
Anthers were introduced in test tubes, immersed in a buffer solution of 10 mL of sodium acetate (pH = 5), and placed in a water bath at 37 ºC throughout the night.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate anthers in sodium acetate buffer

steps.description: Soaked anthers were taken with a dissecting needle and placed in a solution of one drop of demineralized water and one drop of potassium iodine (KI).
The anthers were macerated to release the pollen.

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the viability of pollen grains using image analysis software. It includes capturing images, processing them, and counting viable pollen grains.

steps.description: Each sample was observed with a stereoscope, which is able to capture images by connecting it to a computer through a USB 2.0 cable and proper software.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of pollen samples using stereoscope

steps.description: Images were subjected to treatments: addition, color intensity, and intensity threshold for detection of viable pollen.
steps.searchable_summary: process images for pollen viability detection

steps.description: Total and viable pollen were counted with the software SigmaScan Pro 5.
steps.searchable_summary: count total and viable pollen using SigmaScan Pro 5

steps.description: Percent viable pollen (PVP) was calculated as the quotient of the number of viable pollen grain and the total pollen grains.

methods.description: This method describes the process of determining spikelet sterility by counting vain and full rice grains.

steps.description: Spikelet sterility was determined at harvest on 10 panicles randomly selected on each experimental plot.
steps.searchable_summary: determine spikelet sterility by counting grains

steps.description: Vain and full rice grains were counted and separated.
steps.searchable_summary: count and separate vain and full grains

steps.description: Spikelet sterility was calculated as the quotient between the number of vain grains and the total rice grains.
